scope: Milestone 6: W==D-compare a scheduler-generated FFN gate/up GEMV (cross-lane ds_bpermute) vs the hand-written owned warp GEMV. Research measurement; no default change.
method: standalone clock-pinned in-process INTERLEAVED 3-arm A/B (extra/qk_scheduler_gemv_vs_owned_wd.py); real per-token .item() W==D; NMEAS=30, REPEATS=3; ctx {512,1024,2048,4096}. Only FFN gate/up differs across arms.

arms.sched_lds: Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER=1 WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING=0 MV_ROWS_PER_THREAD=1 (scheduler fp matvec, LDS-tree reduce)
arms.sched_xlane: Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER=1 WARP_REDUCE_LOWERING=1 MV_ROWS_PER_THREAD=1 (scheduler fp matvec, ds_bpermute reduce)

checks.route_detail: owned arm: 72 owned q4k_gemv_warp_12288 kernels, 0 scheduler; sched arms: 0 owned, 18 scheduler gate/up kernels emitting ds_bpermute (sched_xlane)

findings.scheduler_gemv_trails_owned: ~2x slower (50 vs ~100 tok/s whole-decode, gate/up routed). EXPECTED.
findings.cross_lane_neutral_for_this_gemv: ds_bpermute vs LDS-tree = ~0% (-0.08..-0.31%) whole-decode. For a bandwidth-bound GEMV the group-reduce is a tiny fraction; removing LDS+barrier saves ~nothing vs the weight-read cost.
findings.the_gap_is_NOT_the_reduce: The owned kernel's advantage is the dequant lifecycle + packed-Q4_K-word coalesced loads + 4-way block-group-K. The scheduler arm fuses a lazy Q4_K->fp16 dequant (recomputed per token) and lacks those. Cross-lane only fixes the reduce, which is not the bottleneck here.

interpretation: The cross-lane primitive is now scheduler-emittable AND usable in a real model GEMV (route fires, correct) -- the Milestone-5 capability holds in-model. But a scheduler-generated GEMV is far from the hand oracle, and cross-lane is not the lever for it. The next search-space levers (to make search approach the owned kernel) are the Q4_K dequant lifecycle + packed-word memory pattern + block-group-K, NOT the reduce. Matches the DNR-arc caveat (exposing an instruction != reaching owned-kernel perf).
next_levers_for_search_vs_oracle: fused packed-Q4_K dequant in tinygrad ops that reads packed words with coalesced access (vs the lazy fp16 dequant)
block-group-K / occupancy expressed via opts so a scheduler GEMV gets the owned kernel's K-split
per-component cross-lane (handle UPCAST>1 vector reduces) -- but lower priority since the reduce isn't the bottleneck
